edges.evidence_quote: Satan's Choice grew to more than 400 members by 1970, making it the second largest outlaw motorcycle club in the world, behind only the Hells Angels.
edges.context: Satan's Choice was smaller than Hells Angels in size.

edges.evidence_quote: The club was involved in the first major outlaw biker conflict in Canadian history, when it engaged the country's second largest club, the Popeyes, from 1974 to 1976.
edges.context: Satan's Choice fought the Popeyes in a biker conflict from 1974 to 1976.

edges.evidence_quote: Satan's Choice's power began to diminish during the late 1970s, with some of the club's chapters 'patching over' to the Outlaws in 1977.
edges.context: Some Satan's Choice chapters joined the Outlaws in 1977.

edges.evidence_quote: The remaining chapters would eventually become members of the Hells Angels, along with most of the other major outlaw clubs in Ontario, in 2000.
edges.context: Remaining Satan's Choice chapters became Hells Angels members in 2000.

edges.evidence_quote: Harry Paul Barnes of the Black Diamond Riders started a biker war against Satan's Choice.
edges.context: Black Diamond Riders attacked and forced the original Satan's Choice to disband in 1962.

edges.evidence_quote: Phantom Riders president Bernie Guindon subsequently revived the name and patch of the disbanded club as a way to enrage the Black Diamond Riders.
edges.context: Phantom Riders president revived Satan's Choice name; Phantom Riders merged into the new Satan's Choice in 1965.

edges.evidence_quote: a 1965 alliance of four regional motorcycle clubs: the Phantom Riders of Oshawa, the Canadian Lancers of Scarborough, the Throttle Twisters of Preston, and the Wild Ones of Port Credit.
edges.context: Canadian Lancers merged into the new Satan's Choice in 1965.

edges.evidence_quote: a 1965 alliance of four regional motorcycle clubs: the Phantom Riders of Oshawa, the Canadian Lancers of Scarborough, the Throttle Twisters of Preston, and the Wild Ones of Port Credit.
edges.context: Throttle Twisters merged into the new Satan's Choice in 1965.

edges.evidence_quote: a 1965 alliance of four regional motorcycle clubs: the Phantom Riders of Oshawa, the Canadian Lancers of Scarborough, the Throttle Twisters of Preston, and the Wild Ones of Port Credit.
edges.context: Wild Ones merged into the new Satan's Choice in 1965.

edges.evidence_quote: Police say about 12 members of the Golden Hawk Riders and Satan's Choice were out to get even with a musician after he fought with a Golden Hawk Friday and kicked over his motorcycle.
edges.context: Golden Hawk Riders and Satan's Choice fought together against a musician in 1966.

edges.evidence_quote: Guindon held the first national convention of Satan's Choice at a farmhouse in Markham Township just outside of Toronto, which was also attended by the Vagabonds club.
edges.context: Vagabonds attended Satan's Choice's national convention in 1967.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Outlaws MC (1974–1977), Hells Angels MC (1996–2000), Devil's Disciples MC
edges.context: Devil's Disciples listed as an ally in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Hells Angels MC (1974–1995), Outlaws MC (1977–2000), Black Diamond Riders MC, Loners MC, Popeye MC
edges.context: Loners listed as a rival in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Hells Angels MC (1974–1995), Outlaws MC (1977–2000), Black Diamond Riders MC, Loners MC, Popeye MC
edges.context: Hells Angels listed as a rival from 1974 to 1995 in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Hells Angels MC (1974–1995), Outlaws MC (1977–2000), Black Diamond Riders MC, Loners MC, Popeye MC
edges.context: Outlaws listed as a rival from 1977 to 2000 in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Hells Angels MC (1974–1995), Outlaws MC (1977–2000), Black Diamond Riders MC, Loners MC, Popeye MC
edges.context: Black Diamond Riders listed as a rival in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Hells Angels MC (1974–1995), Outlaws MC (1977–2000), Black Diamond Riders MC, Loners MC, Popeye MC
edges.context: Popeye listed as a rival in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Outlaws MC (1974–1977), Hells Angels MC (1996–2000), Devil's Disciples MC
edges.context: Outlaws listed as an ally from 1974 to 1977 in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Outlaws MC (1974–1977), Hells Angels MC (1996–2000), Devil's Disciples MC
edges.context: Hells Angels listed as an ally from 1996 to 2000 in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: The club was involved in the first major outlaw biker conflict in Canadian history, when it engaged the country's second largest club, the Popeyes, from 1974 to 1976.
edges.context: Satan's Choice fought the Popeyes in a biker conflict from 1974 to 1976.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1975, Satan's Choice began an alliance with the Outlaws Motorcycle Club... Under the terms of the agreement, the Outlaws were the exclusive distributors in the United States of the PCPs and methamphetamine manufactured by Choice members in northern Ontario.
edges.context: Satan's Choice allied with the Outlaws in 1975, with the Outlaws distributing drugs for Satan's Choice in the US.

edges.evidence_quote: In October 1969, during a field day in Hamilton, every Satan's Choice member took part as a demonstration of power against the rival Wild Ones club.
edges.context: Satan's Choice demonstrated power against the rival Wild Ones club in 1969.

edges.evidence_quote: This success emboldened elements of Satan's Choice, in particular their Toronto chapter, into sparking a gang war with two rival biker gangs in the city, the Black Diamond Riders MC and the Vagabonds MC.
edges.context: Satan's Choice Toronto chapter sparked a gang war with Black Diamond Riders MC.

edges.evidence_quote: This success emboldened elements of Satan's Choice, in particular their Toronto chapter, into sparking a gang war with two rival biker gangs in the city, the Black Diamond Riders MC and the Vagabonds MC.
edges.context: Satan's Choice Toronto chapter sparked a gang war with Vagabonds MC.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1968, the Hells Angels national president Sonny Barger sent a delegate to Toronto to ask Guindon to join the Hells Angels, a request that was refused.
edges.context: Hells Angels attempted to recruit Satan's Choice in 1968, but were refused.

edges.evidence_quote: The benefits of such a relationship worked to the advantage of both groups. Satan's Choice gained access to a larger support network of clubs throughout the United States and beyond... Meanwhile, the Outlaws gained a strong ally in Ontario...
edges.context: Satan's Choice and Outlaws formed an alliance, with mutual benefits: Satan's Choice got support network, Outlaws gained ally in Ontario.

edges.evidence_quote: This led the Montreal chapter and their Outlaw allies into open conflict with the Hells Angels-backed Popeyes, resulting in a number of casualties on both sides.
edges.context: Satan's Choice (Montreal chapter) and Outlaws fought against Popeyes, who were backed by Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: The Popeyes had targeted the Devil's Disciples biker gang, who were allies of Satan's Choice.
edges.context: Devil's Disciples were allies of Satan's Choice.

edges.evidence_quote: The same underworld struggle drew in the Irish West End Gang on the side of Satan's Choice while the Dubois Brothers gang backed the Popeyes.
edges.context: West End Gang sided with Satan's Choice in the conflict against Popeyes.

edges.evidence_quote: The same underworld struggle drew in the Irish West End Gang on the side of Satan's Choice while the Dubois Brothers gang backed the Popeyes.
edges.context: Dubois Brothers backed the Popeyes in the conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: The Popeyes, who acted as 'muscle' for the Dubois brothers, killed 15 members of the Devil's Disciples between 1974 and 1976, causing the latter gang to disband themselves in January 1976.
edges.context: Popeyes killed 15 Devil's Disciples members, leading to their disbandment.

edges.evidence_quote: In the 1970s, the San Francisco-based Hells Angels were seeking to expand from California into Florida, leading to a biker war with the Chicago-based Outlaws.
edges.context: Hells Angels and Outlaws were in a biker war over expansion into Florida.

edges.evidence_quote: William 'Gatemouth' Edson of the Outlaw chapter in Fort Lauderdale, Florida had been convicted of multiple offenses such as murdering three Hells Angels; torturing a woman for two days because she wore a jacket with an Outlaw patch without permission; and almost beating another woman to death because she was the girlfriend of a Bandidos Motorcycle Club member.
edges.context: Outlaws member Edson assaulted a woman because she was girlfriend of a Bandidos member, indicating hostility.

edges.evidence_quote: The possibility of Satan's Choice 'patching over' to join the Hells Angels ensured that though the Outlaws–Satan's Choice relationship was tense and always difficult, the Outlaws never wanted to apply too much pressure against the Choice least they join the Hells Angels.
edges.context: Satan's Choice considered patching over to Hells Angels, which deterred Outlaws from pressuring them.

edges.evidence_quote: The way his supposed allies, the Outlaws, poached several chapters away from him while he was in prison caused Guindon to have a lasting grudge against the Outlaws, and during the subsequent struggle between the Hells Angels and the Outlaws, Guindon was to lean in a pro-Angel neutrality.
edges.context: After Outlaws poached Satan's Choice chapters, Guindon held a grudge and leaned toward Hells Angels neutrality.

edges.evidence_quote: In the aftermath of the split of 1977, relations between the Outlaws and Satan's Choice were very unfriendly.
edges.context: The text states relations were very unfriendly after the 1977 split, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: Several members of Satan' Choice warned the Golden Hawk Riders, including their sergeant-at-arms, Bill "Heavy" Matiyek, who had a reputation as a hothead, not to go through with the planned "patch over"
edges.context: Satan's Choice warned Golden Hawk Riders not to join the Outlaws, leading to a confrontation.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1981, Satan's Choice made an alliance with the Lobos and the Chosen Few gangs to improve their bargaining power against their rivals, the Outlaws.
edges.context: Satan's Choice allied with Chosen Few in 1981 to counter the Outlaws.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1981, Satan's Choice made an alliance with the Lobos and the Chosen Few gangs to improve their bargaining power against their rivals, the Outlaws.
edges.context: Satan's Choice allied with Lobos in 1981 to counter the Outlaws.

edges.evidence_quote: On 8 September 1983, Guy "Frenchie" Gilbert of Satan's Choice Kitchener chapter was having lunch at Le Petit Bourg restaurant in Longueuil, Quebec with Yves Buteau, the national president of the Hells Angels, to discuss "patching over" to join the Hells Angels.
edges.context: Satan's Choice Kitchener chapter sought to patch over to Hells Angels in 1983.

edges.evidence_quote: A series of police raids in 1983 in Madoc discovered a methamphetamine factory operated jointly by Satan's Choice and the Para-Dice Riders that produced about $3.6 million worth of methamphetamine per month.
edges.context: Satan's Choice and Para-Dice Riders jointly operated a methamphetamine factory in 1983.

edges.evidence_quote: In August 1985, a biker war in Hamilton led to the murder of two Satan's Choice members, Allan Kinloch and Brent Roddick; an Outlaw, James Lewis; and a member of the Red Devils, Michael Carey.
edges.context: Satan's Choice and Red Devils were both involved in the 1985 Hamilton biker war, but the text does not specify a direct relationship between them beyond co-occurrence in the conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: In the summer of 1989, a group of Satan's Choice bikers led by Guindon and Campbell visited the Prairie provinces, meeting with the leaders of los Bravos gang of Winnipeg and the Grim Reapers in Calgary and Lethbridge to form alliances.
edges.context: Satan's Choice met with Los Bravos to form an alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: In the summer of 1989, a group of Satan's Choice bikers led by Guindon and Campbell visited the Prairie provinces, meeting with the leaders of los Bravos gang of Winnipeg and the Grim Reapers in Calgary and Lethbridge to form alliances.
edges.context: Satan's Choice met with Grim Reapers to form an alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: A report by the police Organized Crime Committee from 1989 stated that Satan's Choice, the Vagabonds and the Lobos were working closely to manufacture and distribute methamphetamine.
edges.context: Satan's Choice worked closely with Vagabonds to manufacture and distribute methamphetamine.

edges.evidence_quote: A report by the police Organized Crime Committee from 1989 stated that Satan's Choice, the Vagabonds and the Lobos were working closely to manufacture and distribute methamphetamine.
edges.context: Satan's Choice worked closely with Lobos to manufacture and distribute methamphetamine.

edges.evidence_quote: The feud with the Outlaws led Guindon and his deputy Campbell to look with approval upon the Hells Angels who had driven the Outlaws out of Quebec with much ruthlessness.
edges.context: Satan's Choice had a feud with the Outlaws.

edges.evidence_quote: In the winter of 1990, a group of senior Satan's Choice members led by Guindon and Campbell visited the Hells Angels' clubhouse in Sorel to seek an alliance with the Angels against the Outlaws.
edges.context: Satan's Choice sought an alliance with Hells Angels against the Outlaws.

edges.evidence_quote: On the same night, he was beheaded in his bed by a member of the Loners gang, Frank Passarelli
edges.context: A member of the Loners murdered a Satan's Choice member.

edges.evidence_quote: On 5 September 1992 in Sudbury, the Satan's Choice bikers were involved in a brawl with the Black Diamond Riders in a motel parking lot that put eight of the Riders into the hospital.
edges.context: Satan's Choice fought a brawl with the Black Diamond Riders.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1995, Satan's Choice made an alliance with the Diablos, a club led by a former Choice member, Frank Lenti, resulting in a biker war in the summer of 1995 with the Loners
edges.context: Satan's Choice allied with the Diablos against the Loners.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1995, Satan's Choice made an alliance with the Diablos, a club led by a former Choice member, Frank Lenti, resulting in a biker war in the summer of 1995 with the Loners
edges.context: Diablos were in conflict with the Loners, leading to a biker war.

edges.evidence_quote: Under pressure from the Hells Angels, the Loners came into conflict with the Diablos in the summer of 1995
edges.context: Hells Angels pressured the Loners, leading to conflict with Diablos.

edges.evidence_quote: The club was involved in the first major outlaw biker conflict in Canadian history, when it engaged the country's second largest club, the Popeyes, from 1974 to 1976.
edges.context: Satan's Choice and Popeyes were in a conflict from 1974 to 1976.

edges.evidence_quote: Satan's Choice's power began to diminish during the late 1970s, with some of the club's chapters 'patching over' to the Outlaws in 1977.
edges.context: Some Satan's Choice chapters merged into the Outlaws in 1977.

edges.evidence_quote: Satan's Choice, along with most of the other major Ontario biker gangs, including the Loners, Lobos, and Para-Dice Riders, 'patched over' to the Hells Angels on December 29, 2000.
edges.context: Satan's Choice merged into the Hells Angels on December 29, 2000.

edges.evidence_quote: Satan's Choice, along with most of the other major Ontario biker gangs, including the Loners, Lobos, and Para-Dice Riders, 'patched over' to the Hells Angels on December 29, 2000.
edges.context: Loners merged into Hells Angels on December 29, 2000.

edges.evidence_quote: Satan's Choice, along with most of the other major Ontario biker gangs, including the Loners, Lobos, and Para-Dice Riders, 'patched over' to the Hells Angels on December 29, 2000.
edges.context: Lobos merged into Hells Angels on December 29, 2000.

edges.evidence_quote: Satan's Choice, along with most of the other major Ontario biker gangs, including the Loners, Lobos, and Para-Dice Riders, 'patched over' to the Hells Angels on December 29, 2000.
edges.context: Para-Dice Riders merged into Hells Angels on December 29, 2000.

edges.evidence_quote: Kirby left Satan's Choice in March 1976 and went to work for the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.context: A former Satan's Choice member went to work for 'Ndrangheta in 1976.
